steps.title: Spot the list structure
steps.content: After the blank the sentence continues 'and monitor seasonal ice zones.' Combined with 'measure a wider range of variables' and 'travel to greater depths,' there are three parallel verb phrases that share the subject 'More advanced floats': measure variables, travel to greater depths, and monitor seasonal ice zones.

steps.title: Apply the series-comma rule
steps.content: A series of three items takes commas between items, with 'and' before the final item: 'A, B, and C.'

steps.title: Eliminate by choice
steps.content:
• "variables: travel to greater depths": a colon needs an independent clause before it and treats what follows as elaboration, not as a middle item in a list. ✗
  • "variables, travel to greater depths,": gives the correct series punctuation with a comma after each of the first two items. ✓
  • "variables travel to greater depths,": missing comma after 'variables' fuses the first two list items into one ungrammatical phrase. ✗
  • "variables, travel to greater depths;": a semicolon before 'and' in a simple list of phrases is incorrect; semicolons separate items only when items themselves contain commas. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
